rulings.Case Title: CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, A MUNICIPAL ET AL VS. ANNE KIHAGI ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Enforce Stay Of Receiver Actions Pending Appeal
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 30, 2021 line 1. DEFENDANTS NOTICE OF MOTION TO ENFORCE STAY OF RECEIVER ACTIONS PENDING APPEAL is DENIED as to the relief requested. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: SHANTEL MCCLENDON ET AL VS. THE JOHN STEWART COMPANY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Strike Plaintiff'S First Amended Complaint Or, Alternatively, To Strike The Protected Portions Of Plaintiffs' First Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 30, 2021 line 4. DEFENDANTS THE JOHN STEWART COMPANY, CARLOS URIBE AND ANNA CHUN'S NOTICE OF MOTION TO STRIKE PLAINTIFF'S FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT OR, ALTERNATIVELY, TO STRIKE THE PROTECTED PORTIONS OF PLAINTIFFS' FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT is continued to September 10, 2021. On June 14, 2021 the Court issued a tentative ruling requesting and authorizing supplemental brief limited to case citations re: applicability of relation back doctrine to motion opposition briefs. On July 21, 2021 Plaintiff filed an unauthorized and unresponsive 8-page brief. July 21, 2021 brief is stricken. However, since Plaintiff's counsel suggests that there may be basis for CCP 473(b) relief, Plaintiff counsel may file a motion seeking such relief. Motion shall be set for September 10, 2021 by Plaintiff on CCP 1005, 1013 notice. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). rulings.Case Title: EDWARD KOUNTZE ET AL VS. KALLE KARL-HEINZ PIEPER ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Amended Notice Of And Motion Of Defendant Motion For Reconsideration Of The Courts Order Sustaining In Party And Overruling In Party Defendants Demurrer
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 30, 2021 line 5. PLAINTIFFS EDWARD KOUNTZE AND JEAN BOLDAN'S AMENDED NOTICE OF AND MOTION OF DEFENDANT MOTION FOR RECONSIDERATION OF THE COURTS ORDER SUSTAINING IN PART AND OVERRULING IN PART DEFENDANTS' DEMURRER is GRANTED. The ruling on the demurrer to the complaint is: Defendant's Demurrer to Complaint is SUSTAINED in part and OVERRULED in part. Demurrer based on the argument that claims asserted in this action should have been brought as compulsory cross-complaint in previously filed action is OVERRULED. Claims asserted in this action do not arise out of the same transaction or series of transactions. Demurrer is sustained with leave to amend as to all causes of action for each plaintiff to allege facts in support of each element of each cause of action as to each defendant. First Amended Complaint filed on May 13, 2021 is stricken. Plaintiff shall file and serve its proposed first Amended Complaint in the form attached to the motion for reconsideration no later than July 30, 2021 by 5:00 p.m. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: ANNA ESTRADA VS. SALLY LIU ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Special Motion To Strike
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 30, 2021 line 6. CROSS DEFENDANT EDWARD HIGGINBOTHAM'S NOTICE OF SPECIAL MOTION TO STRIKE is OFF CALENDAR without prejudice for Cross-Defendant's to file a properly briefed motion under Baral v. Schnitt (2016) 1 Cal.5th 376, identifying all allegations to be stricken upon which a protected claim is based. The request to strike the entirety of the cross-complaint and its 12 causes of action without the "claim" analysis under Baral is improper. The Court notes that Cross-Complaint could have been better articulated and that discerning the claims may be not easy, however that is the requirement of the first element of ANTI-SLAPP. Cross-Defendant's ANTI-SLAPP motion will not be considered untimely if it is filed in accordance with this Court's ruling no later than October 1, 2021. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: JAY ZAVERI VS. MOHAMED ABDELGAWAD ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 30, 2021 line 11. DEFENDANT MOHAMED ABDELGAWAD'S FINANCIAL DISTRESS DECLARATION is DENIED. CCP 1179.03 does not provide for relief requested in this motion, i.e. for the Court to determine at this point in the case whether allegations in the complaint regarding the nature of defendant's occupancy are true. The only issue to be addressed on this motion is whether the tenant's failure to return a declaration of COVID-19-related financial distress within the time required by subdivision (g) was the result of mistake, inadvertence, surprise, or excusable neglect, not whether residential tenancy subject to COVID-19 protections existed. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.
